content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
• 428 J Street
• Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916)322-5660
• Fax (916) 322-0886
November 19, 1998
Coralin Feierbach
Belmont City Council
3206 East Laurel Creek Road
Belmont, California 94002
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-98-264
Dear Ms. Feierbach:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’ You are a member of the Belmont City
Council and have requested advice on behalfof the entire city council. Unless you are the
authorized representative for the city council, I cannot advise you because we are unable to give
third party advice. We also do not advise on past conduct. (Regulation 1 8329(b)(2)(A) and
(b)(8)(A).) Therefore, we cannot comment on whether the city has been appropriately applying
the rules for “legally required participation.” However, I am able to discuss this issue with
respect to prospective conduct with your city attorney as the authorized representative for the city
council.
You submitted the following facts: a bond measure is before the council where four of
the five councilmembers are in conflict in voting on various resolutions and ordinances regarding
the measure and on finally placing the measure on the ballot. Two of the councilmembers live
near the property in question and two are realtors who sell property in the area in question.
The bond measure, if passed, will tax all Belmont property owners an assessment to
purchase an area called the San Juan Canyon for open space. The city attorney previously
advised that the four members who have a conflict could draw straws to select two members to
participate, thus achieving a quorum of three councilmembers. You stated that the members are
allowed to draw straws one time and those same people will participate throughout the entire
process. You inquired if it is possible to draw straws each time the council votes on the same
Government Code sections 81000
- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. G-98-264
Page 2
issue. You also stated that the council has drawn straws twice: the first time to establish a
quorum and the second time when a member was sick.
As stated above, we can neither render third party advice nor advise on past conduct.
Therefore, I am enclosing several advice letters and regulations which give guidance regarding
your question.
2
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
SGC:JS :tls
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
D
Enclosures:
Hopkins Advice Letter No. A-82-088
Hill Advice Letter No. A-87-1 10
Heisinger Advice Letter No. A-95-333
Dorsey Advice Letter No. A-92-089
Cohen Advice Letter No. A-94-274
Roche Advice Letter No. G-95-249
Regulations 18707, 18707.1, 18707.2, 18707.3, 18708
2 Please note that the conflict-of-interest regulations were restructured and renumbered in October 1998.
Counsel, Legal Division
I:\Advice Ltrs\98264.wpd

sections.question: Whether city council members with conflicts of interest regarding a bond measure can draw straws to determine participation, and whether straws can be drawn each time the council votes on the same issue, given that four of five councilmembers have conflicts (two live near the property, two are realtors selling property in the area).
sections.question_synthetic: Can conflicted city council members use a straw-drawing process to select which conflicted members participate in voting on a bond measure, and may this process be repeated for each vote on the same issue?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC cannot provide advice on this matter because it was requested on behalf of the city council by a councilmember rather than an authorized representative, and FPPC does not advise on past conduct; FPPC can only discuss prospective conduct with the city attorney as authorized representative. FPPC provides references to prior advice letters and regulations for guidance.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter with limited substantive guidance; FPPC refuses to answer the specific question due to procedural limitations (third-party advice prohibition, no past conduct advice); references multiple enclosures with prior guidance rather than providing original analysis.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether city council members with conflicts of interest regarding a bond measure can draw straws to determine participation, and whether straws can be drawn each time the council votes on the same issue, given that four of five councilmembers have conflicts (two live near the property, two are realtors selling property in the area).

CONCLUSION: FPPC cannot provide advice on this matter because it was requested on behalf of the city council by a councilmember rather than an authorized representative, and FPPC does not advise on past conduct; FPPC can only discuss prospective conduct with the city attorney as authorized representative. FPPC provides references to prior advice letters and regulations for guidance.

Therefore, we cannot comment on whether the city has been appropriately applying the rules for “legally required participation.” However, I am able to discuss this issue with respect to prospective conduct with your city attorney as the authorized representative for the city council. embedding.summary: FPPC declines to provide direct advice to a Belmont City Council member regarding conflict-of-interest rules for a bond measure vote, citing lack of authorization to give third-party advice and inability to advise on past conduct, but indicates it can advise the city attorney on prospective matters and provides reference materials.
